    Detail
    Mar 2026
    Role × Access matrix
    Blueprint §11 · how each role maps to what they can see and do
    Role
    Access tier
    What they can see & do
    Executive / Leadership
    Supervisor tier
    Overview, high-level trends, plan visibility, invoices, documents, major alerts. Read-only.
    Operations Manager
    Supervisor tier
    Executive view plus module drill-downs, backlog inspection, interaction logs, operational analytics.
    Supervisor / QA Lead
    Supervisor tier
    All interactions, transcripts and details, QA scores, exception management, alerts in owned areas.
    Admin
    Admin tier
    Full access. Users & roles, notification prefs, org settings, exports, configuration.
    Internal Support
    Controlled
    Scoped support view per support model or contract. Internal-facing only.
    Agentfuture expansion
    Agent tier
    Only interactions assigned to them. Transcript visibility configurable per client.
    Export controls: single interaction details, copy transcript, download recording (when applicable). Bulk export deferred to Phase 2.
    Callbacks climbing past SLA 12 callbacks unassigned >4h in Scheduling. Auto-route to available Scheduling Coordinators?
    ✕

    Open questions — Scheduling · Overview
    Demo data. Your answers below set what this page surfaces in production.
    1. Are these the right KPIs to lead the page? Affects: which numbers anchor the top of Scheduling.
      Demo shows Appointments Booked, Reschedules, Cancellations, Scheduling Errors, Abandon Rate, Avg Handle Time, and Quality Score.
    2. What turns an alert red, amber, or grey — and who gets it? Affects: when alerts fire and where they land.
      Demo: 4.2% scheduling-error rate flags red, 18-min wait amber, 6.8% abandon amber. Ownership splits between Ops, Compliance, and QA.
    3. What stages does your team use for the booking funnel? Affects: the funnel chart labels and drop-off math.
      Demo: Calls In → Identified → Eligibility → Provider Match → Booked.

    Open questions — Scheduling · Follow-up
    One queue covers Calls Abandoned, ID Lookup, Voicemails, Escalations, and SLA Breaches. Your answers set the policy.
    1. How long before something is past due? Affects: when a row moves into SLA Breaches and how Avg Time to Resolve is calculated.
      Demo uses 4 hours across all five streams.
    2. How many voicemails before we escalate? Affects: when a voicemail row promotes into the Escalations filter.
      Demo leaves 1 per outbound call. 2 attempts? 3?
    3. What pattern flags a caller as a repeat-failure risk? Affects: the Camarena fallback escalation rule.
      Demo: 3+ verification fallback failures in 7 days.

    Open questions — Scheduling · Order Intake
    Pipeline, Patient List, Fax Queue, and Aging Buckets are demo. Your answers set the rules.
    1. How old is "too old" for a referral? Affects: which orders are flagged as aging or blocked.
      Demo uses 0–24h, 1–3 days, 3–7 days, 7+ days.
    2. When does an order move forward — automatically or by hand? Affects: how the pipeline behaves day to day.
      Demo auto-advances when eligibility succeeds; failures park in Errors for review.

    Open questions — Scheduling · Appointments
    Demo counts will be replaced with live data. Your answers set how each one is calculated.
    1. If Grace books a slot and a live agent reschedules it, who gets credit? Affects: how reschedules count toward Grace's automation rate.
      Demo treats them as independent — Grace keeps the original, live agent gets the reschedule.
    2. What counts as an abandoned call? Affects: the Calls Abandoned KPI and the Follow-up list.
      Demo counts hang-ups before booking. Excludes voicemail-only and IVR drop-offs.

    Open questions — Total Interactions
    Total Interactions inherits filters from drill-throughs (Usage & Analytics outcome clicks, Overview KPI clicks) and carries handler / module / outcome chips of its own. Confirming how those compose with the global topbar period.
    1. When a drill-through lands here, do the inherited filters override or merge with the global topbar period? Affects: state continuity across drill-throughs (U&A → Total Interactions, Overview → Total Interactions).
      Demo: drill-throughs show a banner with source + Clear filters; the topbar period (Mar 2026) stays. Confirm: should drill-throughs ever override the topbar period, or is the current "filters apply, period stays global" rule correct?

    Export agent list Coaching queue ↗
    Open questions — Quality Assurance
    v26g introduced a per-finding Visibility tier (Internal review / Client-visible) on flagged interactions, plus matching segmented filters. The rubric below decides what gets surfaced where.
    1. What's the rubric for the Visibility tier on flagged interactions? Affects: which flagged rows surface to your client team vs. stay in internal QA only.
      Demo: HIPAA autofails default to Internal review; everything else defaults to Client-visible. Confirm the rubric, plus whether a third "Suppressed / Hidden" tier is needed (e.g., resolved findings, out-of-scope flags).

    ✦ Analyze Call
    Scoring Summary
    Opening Conversation50 pts
    Communication Skills40 pts
    HIPAA Identity Verification10 pts
    Total: 100 pts
    Reload
    Saved ✓
    Scoring Criteria Configuration
    Edit all fields · Click "Save" to persist · Fully customizable per client
    + Add Criterion
    Opening Conversation
    50 pts ⋮ ▾
    Criterion Name
    Description
    Sub-criteria + Add
    Timing (max 3 seconds to speak)10pt
    Include agent name10pt
    Offer to assist10pt
    Verified Address or email10pt
    Verified Phone number10pt
    Accepted Examples + Add
    ●Agent begins speaking within 3 seconds from the start of the transcript.
    ●Agent speaks before the caller.
    ●Agent does not place the caller on hold or remain silent instead of greeting.
    ●"This is [Agent Name]." / "My name is [Agent Name]." / "You're speaking with [Agent Name]."
    ●"Can you please confirm your address/email?" — If criterion does not apply = pass
    ●"Can you please confirm your phone number?" / "Can you confirm the phone number we have on file?"
    Fail Conditions + Add
    No autofail rules in this category · each fail deducts points within its sub-criterion.
    ✕Agent begins speaking after 3 seconds.
    ✕Caller speaks before the agent.
    ✕Agent does not state their name at any point in the opening.
    ✕Agent does not offer assistance at any point in the opening.
    ✕Scheduling or modifying an appointment without address verification.
    ✕Updating patient information without confirming contact number.
    Communication Skills
    40 pts ⋮ ▾
    Criterion Name
    Description
    Sub-criteria+ Add
    Listened carefully10pt
    Used hold script and checked back in / hold went longer than expected10pt
    Spoke in a respectful manner; no profanity, maintained appropriate conversation10pt
    Managed, paced, and maintained control of the call; didn't stop on the customer while speaking10pt
    Accepted Examples+ Add
    ●The agent's questions and responses match the caller's stated reason for calling.
    ●"May I place you on a brief hold while I check that for you?"
    ●"Thank you for holding." / No hold occurs during the call, or agent is considerate.
    ●The agent uses dignified, professional, and appropriate language.
    Fail Conditions+ Add
    No autofail rules in this category · each fail deducts points within its sub-criterion.
    ✕The agent asks questions that do not align with the caller's stated need.
    ✕The agent places the caller on hold without asking or informing first.
    ✕The agent exceeds 60 seconds on hold without returning to the caller.
    ✕Any use of profanity.
    ✕Ending the call without explanation.
    HIPAA Identity Verification
    10 pts ⋮ ▾
    Criterion Name
    Description
    Max Points
    Pass Rule
    Accepted Examples+ Add
    ●Agent verifies both patient full name and date of birth before scheduling, rescheduling, or canceling an appointment.
    ●Agent confirms patient full name and date of birth prior to accessing or referencing the patient's medical chart.
    ●If identity verification is required and both patient full name and date of birth are verified before proceeding → PASS
    ●Patient voluntarily provides both full name and date of birth at any point before the agent performs a patient-specific action.
    Fail Conditions+ Add
    Contains an Autofail rule · triggering rule zeroes the entire interaction score.
    !AutofailIf criterion "HIPAA Identity Verification" is not met, total call score = 0.
    ✕Agent proceeds with patient-specific action after verifying only one identifier.
    ✕Agent schedules, reschedules, or cancels an appointment without verifying both patient full name and date of birth.
    ✕Agent discusses medical information, results, diagnoses, or provider notes without confirming both full name and date of birth.
    ✕If identity verification is required and both patient full name AND date of birth are not obtained before the agent proceeds → FAIL

    ›
    Phase 1 feedback — Callbacks
    Manual callback policy gates from the Phase 1 review. Items below split into what landed in this build vs. what's still open.
    What we resolved
    1. Who can create a manual callback. Operations Managers, Supervisors, and Admins. Internal Support has visibility only — not a creator. After-hours: Supervisors or Admins handle creation.
    2. Notification on save. Manual callbacks don't auto-notify. The drawer carries a per-callback "Send notification on save" toggle, defaulted off.
    3. Severity setter. Creator picks. Grace validates the choice against the reason code and surfaces a soft hint if the pairing looks off.
    4. Audit trail + retention. Each manual callback (creator, timestamp, reason, updates) lives in Call History and surfaces in a dedicated Manual Callbacks report. Retention 12 months.
    5. Assignment. High-severity callbacks get manually assigned by the creator; standard or low severity round-robin into the source-module's team queue. Creator can override either default.
    What needs a bit more clarification
    1. Severity threshold for manual assignment. Affects: which severities require a manually picked assignee vs. auto-route to a team queue.
      Confirm whether the manual-assign rule applies to Critical only, or to both Critical and Action required. Standard/low (Needs review / Informational) auto-route by default.
    2. Reason-code taxonomy. Affects: the Reason dropdown across manual + automated callbacks.
      Standardized structure and sub-reason / notes pattern confirmed. We'll send our current draft list as a starting point — confirm/edit is faster than building from scratch.
    3. Status set scope. Affects: status pill states + filter chips on the Callback Queue.
      Pending / In Progress / Resolved are confirmed. Should we also surface Dismissed and Escalated as explicit states, or treat them as derived from Resolved + a flag?
    4. Operational-action confirmation step. Affects: friction on Save / Resolve / Reassign / Notify.
      Not addressed in this round. With auto-routing assignment now firing on Save with a blank assignee, a single "Save callback?" summary confirm may be worth the click trade.
    Referrals
    214 active referrals · Mar 2026
    Open Backlog
    214
    ▼ -12 vs last period
    Avg Turnaround
    4.2d
    ▼ -0.4d vs target
    Completion Rate
    71%
    ▼ -4pts vs target
    Aging 30+ Days
    38
    ▲ +7 vs last period
    ✦ Grace Analysis
    46% of incomplete referrals are dropping at the insurance verification step. Missing insurance information at intake is the primary driver — most commonly affecting United Health Care and Ambetter members. Turnaround time on specialist referrals to neurology is running 2.1 days above the practice average.
    Recommended: Update referral intake flow to collect insurance carrier, member ID, and group number before routing. Estimated recovery: 60–80 completions per week.

    Mar 2026 · Records pulls and exchanges

    3.1d
    ▼ -0.3d vs last period
    Exceptions
    12
    Require manual review
    Completed this Period
    156
    ▲ +18 vs last period
    ✦ Grace Analysis
    12 open exceptions are concentrated in requests where the requester type is "third-party legal" — these require authorization verification that is not currently automated. Average completion time for this category is 8.4 days versus the 3.1-day overall average.
    Recommended: Route legal ROI requests to a dedicated queue with a specific authorization checklist. Estimated impact: reduces exception aging by approximately 40%.

    Mar 2026 · Claim and payer activity
    Denials >$500
    5
    ▲ +3 this period
    Total Claims
    342
    ▲ +28 vs last period
    Exceptions
    4.2
    ▲ +2 since yesterday
    Action Required
    8
    ▲ +2 since yesterday
    ✦ Grace Analysis
    5 high-value denials (totaling approximately $4,200) are concentrated in two categories: missing prior authorization documentation and incorrect modifier codes on radiology claims. The denial spike this period correlates with a coding change applied to the Imaging workflow on March 30th.
    Recommended: Review modifier code changes from Mar 30 against payer requirements. Resubmit affected claims within the 90-day timely filing window. Estimated recovery: $3,600–$4,200.

    Phase 1 scope note: This stub wires the Documents IA per Blueprint §2 and surfaces the content buckets called out in §10 (plan, invoices, SLA, SOW, legal, implementation artifacts, support guides). Actual uploads, PDF previews, signature flows, and entitlement rollups land in Phase 2–4 per the commercial roadmap.

    5 Grace agents included · soft-cap with usage notifications, overages auto-billed at $/agent

    Portal seats used
    7 / —
    Seat cap pending plan definition
    Phase 1 scope note: Subscription is view-only in Phase 1. Plan changes, agent additions, and overage purchases route through your Customer Success contact. Soft-cap policy avoids interruption: usage notifications fire as you approach the entitlement, and overages auto-bill at the contracted per-agent rate. Invoices live here as the system of record; payment is processed externally (Stripe). SLA and SOW documents stay in Documents and are linked from the Plan details panel above. Self-service plan management is queued for Phase 2.

    Intake funnel · Mar 2026
    Received → identified → routed → terminal outcome · per Mermaid intake flows
    Received
    4,218 · 100%
    Inbound + outbound · all intake entry points
    ▼ 6.0% Audio / language capture failure
    Intent identified
    3,967 · 94.0%
    Language + reason captured · ready for routing
    ▼ 4.1% After-hours or unsupported intent
    Routed to agent
    3,791 · 89.9%
    Gating rules applied · Scheduling / Front Desk / Referrals / etc.
    ▼ 2.7% Dropped or timed out mid-conversation
    Terminal outcome
    3,677 · 87.2%
    Resolved without human escalation · 6 outcome categories
    Drop-off: 6.0% at intent capture, 4.1% at routing, 2.7% at outcome. See funnel details for per-stage Mermaid trace.
    ?
    Open questions · awaiting client input
    Per Blueprint §8 — items below are genuinely client-gated. Resolved design calls have been logged and removed from this view.
    Client input
    Grace-impact / ROI framing — input-only, or built-in calculator? What executive-reporting view should U&A carry.
    412 Grace-handled hours surface today as raw input for the client's own ROI model — finance owns the assumption (fully-loaded agent $/hour). Should U&A add a built-in ROI calculator that takes a $/hour input and returns a savings number, or stay input-only and route ROI to a separate exec view in a later phase? Phased decision — affects whether the Productivity input chip on the Grace Insight bar gains a "Model savings" CTA.
    Phase 2 scope note: This scaffold implements Blueprint §8 (trend, breakdowns, funnel, utilization, comparisons, productivity input). Numbers are deterministic rollups of the Mar 2026 interaction set already used by Overview, Scheduling, Front Desk, and Subscription. Filters and granularity toggles log via graceRecordAction pending backend wiring in Phase 2.

    Available
    Scheduling
    Voice agent
    Scheduling Agent Overview
    Automates appointment booking, rescheduling, and cancellations via inbound calls. Integrates with your EHR scheduling system.
    Available
    Front Desk
    Voice agent
    Front Desk Agent Overview
    Handles call routing, basic intake, and patient triage. Fastest to deploy with minimal configuration required. Ideal starting point.
    Available
    Referrals
    Voice + backend workflow
    Referrals Agent Overview
    Manages referral intake, status tracking, and specialist coordination. Covers inbound calls and backend fax and portal workflows.
    Available
    Billing
    Backend workflow
    Billing Agent Overview
    Automates billing workflows, claim status checks, and patient balance inquiries. Backend automation without live voice handling.
    Available
    Quality Assurance
    Backend workflow
    QA Agent Overview
    Scores and analyzes call recordings against your QA criteria. Works standalone with manual uploads or integrates with live telephony.
    Early Access
    Revenue Cycle Mgmt
    Backend workflow
    RCM Agent Overview
    End-to-end RCM automation covering denials, prior auth follow-up, and payment posting. Currently in controlled early access with select practices.
    Request sent. The VitalCX team will follow up within 2 business days.

    Live Translation — Coming Soon
    Real-time interpretation for patient calls in 40+ languages. Reduces escalations to bilingual live agents and extends reach to underserved patients.
    You are on the list. We will reach out as soon as Live Translation is available.
